title: Remove generated ioprojectatomicpodman.go from Git?
body:
We've tracked it since #669, but it's noisy:

```console
$ git log --oneline --stat=80 cmd/podman/varlink/ioprojectatomicpodman.go
d870c25 implement varlink commit
 cmd/podman/varlink/ioprojectatomicpodman.go | 1614 ++++++++++++++-------------
 1 file changed, 823 insertions(+), 791 deletions(-)
834f1f6 varlink info
 cmd/podman/varlink/ioprojectatomicpodman.go | 1741 +++++++++++++++------------
 1 file changed, 942 insertions(+), 799 deletions(-)
a74107b alphabetize the varlink methods, types, and errors in the docs
 cmd/podman/varlink/ioprojectatomicpodman.go | 1615 ++++++++++++++-------------
 1 file changed, 808 insertions(+), 807 deletions(-)
2526355 Generate varlink API documentation automatically
 cmd/podman/varlink/ioprojectatomicpodman.go | 2673 +++++++++++++++++----------
 1 file changed, 1700 insertions(+), 973 deletions(-)
8dfebd4 varlink containers
 cmd/podman/varlink/ioprojectatomicpodman.go | 1384 +++++++++++++++++----------
 1 file changed, 863 insertions(+), 521 deletions(-)
7a0a855 Fix misc stuff found by jhonce
 cmd/podman/varlink/ioprojectatomicpodman.go | 980 ++++++++++++++--------------
 1 file changed, 495 insertions(+), 485 deletions(-)
a824186 Use buildah commit and bud in podman
 cmd/podman/varlink/ioprojectatomicpodman.go | 972 ++++++++++++++--------------
 1 file changed, 486 insertions(+), 486 deletions(-)
39a7a77 varlink images
 cmd/podman/varlink/ioprojectatomicpodman.go | 1571 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 1 file changed, 1571 insertions(+)
```

because some entries seem to be randomly ordered (e.g. type definitions are not alphabetized).  Since I don't see docs anywhere claiming you can `go get github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pkg/podman`, can we drop it from version control and rely on [the existing `Makefile` rule][1] to ensure we have a fresh copy at build time?

cc @baude, since you've been doing all the regeneration work ;).

[1]: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/blob/4704c138ae7f3c98c3fddba2bc442d61f4eb1f28/Makefile#L265-L266

comments.body:
@baude I know there's a reason we checked it in, but I'm forgetting right now?

I would really love to see the Varlink generator changed to consistently order definitions so we cut down on the churn.
we dont want this to be generated during package building.  it makes things too complicated.  given the noiseyness, a bug against varlink would be appropriate.
